title: Determine Whether Hospital Use Matches Reject Handling or AGV Initialization
summary: Use the training explanation of hospital purpose to interpret why an AGV is being routed through the hospital area. The source describes hospital use for reject handling when an AGV cannot tip, for initializing new AGVs, and as a home-base style location, so hospital routing alone should not be interpreted as meaning the AGV is "sick."
when_to_use: Use when an AGV is observed or reported as being routed through the hospital area and the user needs to interpret whether that routing matches the training-described purposes of reject handling, initialization of a new AGV, or home-base style handling.
not_for: Do not use this runbook to diagnose a specific AGV fault state beyond the source-described meanings of hospital use.
Do not use this runbook to conclude that an AGV is "sick" based only on hospital routing.
Do not use this runbook as a corrective recovery procedure, because the source provides interpretation of hospital purpose rather than a full corrective workflow.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Interpret observed hospital routing using the training-described meanings and avoid unsupported conclusions.

safety_notes: This is an interpretive reference procedure only; the source does not provide physical intervention steps.
Do not assume undocumented fault states from hospital routing alone.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the training explanation of hospital purpose
Ability to observe or confirm that the AGV is routed through the hospital area

access_required: Access to the training explanation of hospital purpose
system_preconditions: An AGV is observed or confirmed to be routed through the hospital area rather than directly tipping or charging.

visual_references.description: Training slide showing modularized flow between tipper, hospital, and charger areas, including hospital entrance and exit queue concepts and transcript support for reject, initialization, and home-base use.

steps.title: Confirm hospital routing is the observed condition
steps.purpose: Establish that the interpretation applies to hospital routing specifically.
steps.instruction: Identify that the AGV is being routed through the hospital area rather than directly tipping or charging.
steps.expected_result: The observed condition is confirmed as hospital routing.
steps.healthy_condition: The AGV path or reported condition clearly involves the hospital area.
steps.failure_condition: The AGV is not actually being routed through the hospital area, or the routing cannot be confirmed from the available observation.

actions.label: Identify hospital routing

steps.title: Compare the situation to source-described hospital purposes
steps.purpose: Match the observed routing to the meanings explicitly described in the training source.
steps.instruction: Compare the observed situation to the source-described hospital purposes: reject handling when an AGV cannot tip at the tipper, initialization of a new AGV, or home-base style handling.
steps.expected_result: The observed hospital routing is compared against the source-supported reasons for hospital use.
steps.healthy_condition: The observed situation aligns with one of the source-described hospital purposes.
steps.failure_condition: The observed situation does not align with reject handling, initialization of a new AGV, or home-base style handling.

actions.label: Compare to hospital purposes
actions.exact_action: Compare the observed situation to the source-described hospital purposes: reject handling when an AGV cannot tip at the tipper, initialization of a new AGV, or home-base style handling.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Hospital flow depiction and training context describing reject handling, initialization, and home-base behavior.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if hospital routing is observed but does not align with the source-described reject or initialization/home-base uses.

source_evidence.text: Compare the observed situation to the source-described hospital purposes: reject handling when an AGV cannot tip at the tipper, initialization of a new AGV, or home-base style handling.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Transcript says hospitals are used when an AGV cannot tip at the tipper, describes this as a reject state/detection, and says the hospital is used to initialize new AGVs and acts like a home base.

The training segment describes the hospital as a reject-related area used when an AGV cannot tip, and also as a home-base location used to initialize new AGVs and support shutdown-related AGV changes.

steps.title: Confirm hospital use does not by itself indicate a sick AGV
steps.purpose: Prevent unsupported interpretation of hospital routing as a generic AGV fault state.
steps.instruction: Verify from the source wording that hospital use does not by itself mean the AGV is "sick."
steps.expected_result: The interpretation remains limited to the source-supported meanings of hospital use.
steps.healthy_condition: Hospital routing is treated as potentially reject-related or initialization/home-base related, not automatically as a sickness indicator.
steps.failure_condition: Hospital routing is being interpreted as meaning the AGV is "sick" without additional source support.

actions.label: Check interpretation boundary
actions.exact_action: Verify from the source wording that hospital use does not by itself mean the AGV is "sick."

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop interpretation if the only basis for concluding the AGV is "sick" is that it is routed through the hospital.

source_evidence.text: Verify from the source wording that hospital use does not by itself mean the AGV is 'sick.'
Do not conclude that an AGV is 'sick' based only on hospital routing, because the source explicitly distinguishes hospital use from that meaning.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Transcript says hospitals are used when an AGV cannot tip at the tipper, describes this as a reject state/detection, and says the hospital is used to initialize new AGVs and acts like a home base.

steps.title: Record the source-supported interpretation

steps.failure_condition: No source-supported interpretation can be recorded from the observed situation.

steps.title: Escalate when hospital routing does not match source-described purposes
steps.purpose: Prevent unsupported conclusions when the observed routing cannot be explained by the training source.
steps.instruction: If the observed reason for hospital routing cannot be matched to the source-described purposes, stop interpretation and escalate.
steps.expected_result: Unsupported interpretations are stopped and the issue is escalated for further review.
steps.healthy_condition: Escalation occurs when the observed routing cannot be matched to reject handling or initialization/home-base behavior.
steps.failure_condition: The user continues interpreting hospital routing beyond what the source supports.

actions.label: Stop and escalate
actions.exact_action: If the observed reason for hospital routing cannot be matched to the source-described purposes, stop interpretation and escalate.

success_criteria: The observed hospital routing is interpreted using only the source-described meanings of reject handling, initialization of a new AGV, or home-base style handling.
No unsupported conclusion is made that hospital routing alone means the AGV is "sick."
Cases that do not match the source-described purposes are escalated.
healthy_conditions: Hospital routing can be reasonably matched to reject handling when an AGV cannot tip at the tipper.
Hospital routing can be reasonably matched to initialization of a new AGV.
Hospital routing can be reasonably matched to home-base style handling described by the source.
failure_conditions: Hospital routing cannot be matched to any source-described purpose.
Hospital routing is interpreted as a sickness indicator without source support.
The observed condition cannot be confirmed as hospital routing.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if hospital routing is observed but does not align with the source-described reject or initialization/home-base uses.
Escalate if additional diagnosis is needed beyond the training source's interpretive explanation.
